# UC-18.1.18 · APIC Cluster Replication Latency

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We clock how far behind each policy controller falls when copying its shared rulebook, and we ring the bell early if one drifts long enough that everyday pushes could trust stale settings.*

---

## Description

Detects sustained **APIC** policy-database replication lag and unhealthy **dbgrReplStatus** / **infraWiNode** combinations—surfing field-normalized delay metrics, pending replica pressure, fault/syslog corroboration, and audit echoes before controllers diverge far enough to stall automation or invite asymmetric policy views.

## Value

Replication delay is the quiet preamble to **split-brain** class incidents: tenants keep forwarding while **APIC** teammates disagree on which contract revision won. Surfacing **dbgr** lag beside **UC-18.1.7** quorum math lets operations drain risky changes, pause orchestration, or fail leaders **before** helpdesks blame applications for mysterious **EPG** drift.

## Implementation

Enable **4022** feeds for **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**; schedule **1546** pulls for **`dbgrReplStatus`**/**`infraWiNode`** into **`cisco:aci:replication`** and land **`cluster_health`** slices as **`cisco:aci:cluster_health`**. Maintain **`FIELDALIAS`**, **`aci_controller_inventory`**, and **`aci_repl_baseline.csv`**; save **`aci_uc1818_replication_latency`** with **5–10m** cadence and **dn_lc** throttles; pair dashboards with optional **7777** macros.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) on Search Heads and indexers that parse `cisco:aci:syslog`, `cisco:aci:audit`, and `cisco:aci:fault` so **FIELDALIAS** maps survive TA upgrades and **`repl_delay_ms`** never silently nulls after a rev bump.
- Layer **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**`rest_ta`**, Splunkbase [**1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) or a hardened HTTPS worker that performs **`POST /api/aaaLogin.json`**, **`POST /api/aaaRefresh.json`**, then polls **`GET /api/node/class/dbgrReplStatus.json`**, **`GET /api/node/class/infraWiNode.json`**, and **`GET /api/node/class/cluster_health.json`** on 60–300s cadence—emit **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:replication`** for **dbgrReplStatus** rows and **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:cluster_health`** for summarized backlog hints.
- Optional **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (Splunkbase [**7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) ships sample views; still normalize sourcetypes with this document before promoting tiles to executives.
- Grant automation RBAC read paths covering **dbgrReplStatus**, **infraWiNode**, **infraCont**, and **topSystem**; never share passwords with GUI admins whose sessions invalidate **aaaRefresh** cookies mid-collection.
- Budget ingest: each APIC can return multiple **dbgrReplStatus** children per poll—roughly 2–6 KB per controller per poll before aggressive **rsp-subtree** scopes; widen intervals if **HTTP 429** appears when operators live in Visore.
- Record calm-week medians for **`repl_delay_ms`** per site; VM-backed controllers often sit higher than **APIC-M/L** appliances—persist baselines in `aci_repl_baseline.csv` for adaptive alert math in Step 4.

Step 1 Configure data collection
Point **rest_ta** stanzas at **`https://<apic>/api/node/class/dbgrReplStatus.json`** and **`https://<apic>/api/node/class/infraWiNode.json`**, tagging **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:replication`**, setting **host** to the APIC FQDN or VIP, and retaining **dn**, delay/status attributes, **id**, and **modTs** when present. Normalize camelCase vendor keys with **FIELDALIAS** so **`repl_delay_ms`**, **`repl_status`**, and **`replicas_pending`** line up across software trains.

Mirror broader health counters into **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:cluster_health`**—this lane complements (does not replace) UC-18.1.7’s **`cisco:aci:cluster`** feed and highlights compaction or backlog decay when **dbgr** slows.

Keep APIC syslog on `cisco:aci:syslog` via the TA so **facility=local6** strings mentioning replication, shards, standby, quorum, or partition survive HTTPS pauses. Retain `cisco:aci:fault` because **faultInst** rows occasionally carry the only structured code for a **dbgr** stall.

Land **`aaaModLR`** and **`configJob`** trails on `cisco:aci:audit` with minimal filtering so Splunk can pair latency spikes to approved maintenance.

Verification: `| metadata type=sourcetypes index=cisco_aci | search sourcetype=cisco:aci:*` should show the five sourcetype families used here; **`| walklex type=field index=cisco_aci field=dn`** should populate **uni/controller/node-*** DNs shortly after polling begins.

Step 2 Create the search
Save the SPL below as `aci_uc1818_replication_latency`, run in Verbose mode first, then clone a baseline-aware variant that joins `aci_repl_baseline.csv` once samples mature.

```spl
index=cisco_aci earliest=-6h@m
(
  sourcetype="cisco:aci:replication"
  OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_health"
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(dbgr|repl|replica|replication|shard|consensus|lag|split|partition)"))
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(dbgr|replication|replica|split|consensus|lag|shard|pending)"))
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(dbgrRepl|infraWiNode|infraCluster|cluster_health|replica)"))
)
| eval collector_tag=coalesce(collector_id, rest_stanza, input_id, "default")
| eval ctrl_node=lower(trim(coalesce(apic_fqdn, apic_host, host, "")))
| eval dn_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(dn, mo_dn, distinguished_name, infraWiNode_dn, "")))
| eval repl_delay_ms=tonumber(coalesce(repl_delay_ms, replication_delay_ms, replDelay, replLagMs, dbReplDelay, delay_ms, lag_ms, "0"))
| eval repl_status=lower(trim(coalesce(replStatus, repl_state, replication_state, dbgr_state, "")))
| eval wi_oper=lower(trim(coalesce(oper_state, operState, wiOperState, "")))
| eval pending_rep=tonumber(coalesce(replicas_pending, replicasPending, "0"))
| eval synced_rep=tonumber(coalesce(replicas_synced, replicasSynced, "0"))
| eval mo_class=lower(trim(coalesce(moClass, className, "")))
| eval is_structured=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:replication" OR sourcetype=="cisco:aci:cluster_health",1,0)
| eval is_narrative=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault" OR sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog",1,0)
| eval is_audit=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:audit",1,0)
| eval band=case(repl_delay_ms<=0 OR isnull(repl_delay_ms),"none", repl_delay_ms<200,"lt200ms", repl_delay_ms<500,"200_499ms", repl_delay_ms<1000,"500_999ms", 1==1,"ge1000ms")
| eval state_sev=case(match(repl_status,"(?i)(out|down|fault|stall|unsync|unknown|fail|error)"),3, match(wi_oper,"(?i)(down|unknown|fail|offline)"),3, len(repl_status)>0 AND NOT match(repl_status,"(?i)(ok|sync|up|idle|insync|in-sync|active|healthy)"),2, true(),0)
| eval lag_sev=case(repl_delay_ms>=1500,3, repl_delay_ms>=750,2, repl_delay_ms>=500,1, true(),0)
| eval pending_sev=if(pending_rep>=12,3,if(pending_rep>=8,2,if(pending_rep>=6,1,0)))
| eval evidence_score=is_narrative+is_audit
| eval composite=max(state_sev,lag_sev,pending_sev)+if(is_structured==1 AND lag_sev>=1,1,0)+if(evidence_score>0 AND lag_sev>=1,1,0)
| where composite>=4 OR (lag_sev>=2 AND is_structured==1) OR (pending_sev>=2 AND state_sev>=2) OR (pending_rep>=10 AND is_structured==1)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(repl_delay_ms) as repl_ms_peak max(lag_sev) as lag_class max(state_sev) as state_class max(pending_rep) as pending_peak min(synced_rep) as synced_min max(composite) as composite_peak dc(ctrl_node) as controller_hops values(band) as band_mix values(sourcetype) as sourcetype_lane dc(collector_tag) as collector_tags latest(repl_status) as repl_row latest(wi_oper) as wi_row values(mo_class) as mo_classes sum(is_structured) as structured_hits sum(is_narrative) as narrative_hits sum(is_audit) as audit_hits by _time dn_lc
| lookup local=true aci_controller_inventory dn_lc OUTPUT site_code fabric_name owning_team
| where repl_ms_peak>=500 OR composite_peak>=5 OR pending_peak>=8
| sort - repl_ms_peak composite_peak
| head 150
| table _time dn_lc site_code fabric_name repl_ms_peak lag_class state_class pending_peak synced_min composite_peak structured_hits narrative_hits audit_hits sourcetype_lane collector_tags mo_classes repl_row wi_row owning_team
```

#### Understanding this SPL
- The union spans `cisco:aci:replication`, `cisco:aci:cluster_health`, `cisco:aci:fault`, `cisco:aci:syslog`, and `cisco:aci:audit` so REST evidence, mnemonic syslog, structured faults, and change records agree before you page.
- `is_structured`, `is_narrative`, and `is_audit` encode transport roles—handy when auditors ask how Splunk distinguished noise from consensus risk.
- Severity evals (`lag_sev`, `state_sev`, `pending_sev`) collapse multi-metric MO XML into `composite_peak` for front-line responders; shift cutoffs using `aci_repl_baseline.csv` rather than editing SPL weekly.
- Five-minute **`bin _time`** windows align with common REST cadences yet still capture ISSU soak spikes when **`max(repl_delay_ms)`** jumps inside one bucket.
- The **`lookup aci_controller_inventory`** enrichment is optional but protects multi-site fabrics from paging the wrong city; keep **dn_lc** keys lowercase to match MO formatting.

Step 3 Validate
- In **Visore** or **APIC CLI**, compare live **dbgrReplStatus** to **`repl_ms_peak`** for the same **`dn_lc`**—**Splunk** lag should stay within twice your poll interval.
- Cross-check **UC-18.1.7**: **`pending_peak`** here should trend with **`replicas_pending_max`** there; divergence hints duplicate collectors or bad **FIELDALIAS** on pending counters.
- During a quiet maintenance window, run **`stats count by fault_code`** on `cisco:aci:fault` filtered to dbgr keywords—expect near-zero; spikes prove syslog/fault correlation is real.
- For rows with **`composite_peak>=5`**, pull **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** events ±10 minutes and confirm **`CHG`** coverage before calling a sev-1.

Step 4 Operationalize
Ship a dashboard titled **APIC Replication Guard** with: (a) timechart of **`repl_ms_peak`** by **`site_code`**; (b) single value counting controllers over **750ms** in the trailing hour; (c) sortable table of **`dn_lc`**, **`pending_peak`**, **`composite_peak`**, and **`sourcetype_lane`**; (d) drilldown to raw events using token **`tok_dn`**.

Schedule `aci_uc1818_replication_latency` every 5–10 minutes over **`earliest=-6h@m`**, trigger on **number of results > 0**, throttle by `dn_lc` for 30–60 minutes, and suppress matches from `maintenance_aci.csv`.

Route sev-2 pages to the ACI platform bridge; send sev-3 email when `audit_hits` is high but `structured_hits` stays flat so automation teams fix change fallout without executive wakeups.

Catalog ownership for `aci_controller_inventory`, `aci_repl_baseline.csv`, and `maintenance_aci.csv`, and require unique `collector_tag` values per REST stanza so duplication shows up in `collector_tags`.

Step 5 Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Visualization: use a dual-axis chart—stepped lines for `repl_ms_peak` per `fabric_name` plus columns for `pending_peak`. Color **<300ms** green, **300–749ms** amber, **≥750ms** red. Add a parallel panel listing `cisco:aci:syslog` excerpts filtered to replication vocabulary so engineers read English sentences next to milliseconds.

Alert design: keep the primary saved-search alert above. Add a secondary alert that subtracts **`baseline_p95_ms`** from `aci_repl_baseline.csv` so historically noisy VM sites stop flapping at night—start **`adaptive_delta`** around **150** ms above baseline. Add a tertiary alert when **`structured_hits=0`** but **`narrative_hits>5`** to catch parser regressions before REST silence hides real stalls.

Troubleshooting cues: zero **`repl_ms_peak`** while **APIC GUI** shows lag means delay attributes never mapped—reopen **FIELDALIAS** against raw JSON. **`controller_hops>1`** inside one bucket suggests duplicate **Heavy Forwarders**; insert **`dedup _time dn_lc keepest=last`** ahead of **`stats`** if needed. High **`audit_hits`** with zero structured rows during **ISSU** is often expected—demand **`CHG`** proof before escalation. Null **`site_code`** means the inventory lookup is stale after **RMA**—refresh **`aci_controller_inventory`**. **HTTP 401** spam in **`splunkd.log`** on the **REST** host means credentials or **aaaRefresh** skew. If **`pending_peak`** freezes while **`repl_ms_peak`** falls, suspect collector stall, not healing—return to **UC-18.1.7** for leadership churn.

Governance: document beside UC-18.1.7 when teams must trust cluster backlog counters versus pure **dbgr** delay so bridges know which saved search leads during gray failures.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci earliest=-6h@m
(
  sourcetype="cisco:aci:replication"
  OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_health"
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(dbgr|repl|replica|replication|shard|consensus|lag|split|partition)"))
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(dbgr|replication|replica|split|consensus|lag|shard|pending)"))
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(dbgrRepl|infraWiNode|infraCluster|cluster_health|replica)"))
)
| eval collector_tag=coalesce(collector_id, rest_stanza, input_id, "default")
| eval ctrl_node=lower(trim(coalesce(apic_fqdn, apic_host, host, "")))
| eval dn_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(dn, mo_dn, distinguished_name, infraWiNode_dn, "")))
| eval repl_delay_ms=tonumber(coalesce(repl_delay_ms, replication_delay_ms, replDelay, replLagMs, dbReplDelay, delay_ms, lag_ms, "0"))
| eval repl_status=lower(trim(coalesce(replStatus, repl_state, replication_state, dbgr_state, "")))
| eval wi_oper=lower(trim(coalesce(oper_state, operState, wiOperState, "")))
| eval pending_rep=tonumber(coalesce(replicas_pending, replicasPending, "0"))
| eval synced_rep=tonumber(coalesce(replicas_synced, replicasSynced, "0"))
| eval mo_class=lower(trim(coalesce(moClass, className, "")))
| eval is_structured=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:replication" OR sourcetype=="cisco:aci:cluster_health",1,0)
| eval is_narrative=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault" OR sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog",1,0)
| eval is_audit=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:audit",1,0)
| eval band=case(repl_delay_ms<=0 OR isnull(repl_delay_ms),"none", repl_delay_ms<200,"lt200ms", repl_delay_ms<500,"200_499ms", repl_delay_ms<1000,"500_999ms", 1==1,"ge1000ms")
| eval state_sev=case(match(repl_status,"(?i)(out|down|fault|stall|unsync|unknown|fail|error)"),3, match(wi_oper,"(?i)(down|unknown|fail|offline)"),3, len(repl_status)>0 AND NOT match(repl_status,"(?i)(ok|sync|up|idle|insync|in-sync|active|healthy)"),2, true(),0)
| eval lag_sev=case(repl_delay_ms>=1500,3, repl_delay_ms>=750,2, repl_delay_ms>=500,1, true(),0)
| eval pending_sev=if(pending_rep>=12,3,if(pending_rep>=8,2,if(pending_rep>=6,1,0)))
| eval evidence_score=is_narrative+is_audit
| eval composite=max(state_sev,lag_sev,pending_sev)+if(is_structured==1 AND lag_sev>=1,1,0)+if(evidence_score>0 AND lag_sev>=1,1,0)
| where composite>=4 OR (lag_sev>=2 AND is_structured==1) OR (pending_sev>=2 AND state_sev>=2) OR (pending_rep>=10 AND is_structured==1)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(repl_delay_ms) as repl_ms_peak max(lag_sev) as lag_class max(state_sev) as state_class max(pending_rep) as pending_peak min(synced_rep) as synced_min max(composite) as composite_peak dc(ctrl_node) as controller_hops values(band) as band_mix values(sourcetype) as sourcetype_lane dc(collector_tag) as collector_tags latest(repl_status) as repl_row latest(wi_oper) as wi_row values(mo_class) as mo_classes sum(is_structured) as structured_hits sum(is_narrative) as narrative_hits sum(is_audit) as audit_hits by _time dn_lc
| lookup local=true aci_controller_inventory dn_lc OUTPUT site_code fabric_name owning_team
| where repl_ms_peak>=500 OR composite_peak>=5 OR pending_peak>=8
| sort - repl_ms_peak composite_peak
| head 150
| table _time dn_lc site_code fabric_name repl_ms_peak lag_class state_class pending_peak synced_min composite_peak structured_hits narrative_hits audit_hits sourcetype_lane collector_tags mo_classes repl_row wi_row owning_team
```

## Visualization

(1) **Timechart** of **`repl_ms_peak`** per **`site_code`**. (2) **Single value** for controllers above **750ms** in the trailing hour. (3) **Table** with **`dn_lc`**, **`pending_peak`**, **`composite_peak`**, **`sourcetype_lane`**, **`owning_team`**. (4) **Overlay** syslog replication keyword counts for human-readable context.

## Known False Positives

**repl_poll_cadence_smoothing** — When HTTPS collectors poll dbgrReplStatus slower than fault narratives, Splunk can miss sub-minute spikes Visore already shows—tighten rest_ta intervals or keep cisco:aci:syslog blended before assuming replicas are healthy.

**aci_full_policy_import_window** — Bulk imports or contract compiles legitimately stretch replication queues while APIC stays sound—tie composite_peak timelines to approved CHG or Ansible job IDs and suppress until pending_peak clears without state_sev escalation.

**ha_leader_transition_buffer** — Leader moves and standby promotions reshape dbgr views for minutes even when sessions converge—require fault codes or mixed sourcetype_lane evidence before sev-1 pages during maintenance_aci.csv windows.

**vmfs_metadata_storm** — ESXi snapshot merges or datastore rescans stall VM-APIC disks, inflating repl_delay_ms without leaf symptoms—check hypervisor calendars and infraWiNode oper transitions before blaming switching.

**mirrored_rest_collector_duplication** — Two rest_ta instances on the same VIP double pending tallies—partition on collector_tag and dedup dn_lc per poll when collector_tags repeats across bins.

**nms_wan_latency_to_passive_site** — Stretched or DR-separated APIC peers over high RTT WAN links can show higher delays on secondary sites—add site_role=primary|dr to aci_controller_inventory and maintain separate baselines instead of one global millisecond gate.

**snmp_trap_only_without_mo_class** — Trap-only noise may mention replication without dbgrReplStatus rows—treat narrative_hits as advisory until structured cisco:aci:replication returns, routing tickets to parser owners instead of fabric bridge calls during long overnight maintenance windows.
